warnings: This sheet (A-14) presents 3 door types (D1, D2, D3, circle symbols) across the top row and 5 window types (W1-W5, hexagon symbols) below/beside them — same total count (3 doors, 5 windows) as house #3's equivalent sheet.

Nearly all dimensions and material specs (aluminum frame/white powder coat, 6mm/4mm glass, wood door 2x4 frame, PVC bathroom door, W5's stair-landing note) are IDENTICAL to house #3's equivalent A-14 sheet — strongly cross-confirms both readings, consistent with the shared government housing-program template. The one exception is W3: this house's W3 is 1.40 x 2.50 (2.00+0.50 height split) vs house #3's 1.40 x 2.30 (1.80+0.50) — a genuine 0.20m per-house difference, not a misread default.

D/W numbering re-derived by position/type since the raw sheet's own per-cell symbol numbering restarts independently for doors (1,2,3) vs windows (1,2,3,4,5) and would collide if used verbatim as element_id — same convention used for house #3's equivalent sheet.

W5 lights the ST.3 main switchback stairwell per its '▼ระดับชานพักบันได' note (this house's main stair being ST.3 per the emerging cross-page evidence — see _หน้า06/07/13/14_floor_plan_and_section files), analogous to how W5 lit ST.2's stairwell in house #3.
